mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that compresses corporate profit margins and triggers negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances when the unemployment rate surpasses the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms robust consumer spending that drives corporate earnings expansion and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 60 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production signals immediate supply chain constraints that compress short-term equity valuations and generate negative weekly returns.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of periods following a monthly decline in industrial production.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly consumer price index readings above 240 force expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples and generates negative returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in greater than 65 percent of months where the consumer price index surpasses 240.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that boosts wage growth and corporate revenues, driving positive quarterly returns.
failure_condition: The employment-unemployment divergence signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth above 2.0 percent annualized confirms economic expansion that supports near-term equity price appreciation over the following week.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns following positive GDP prints are negative in greater than 55 percent of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing inflation signals supply-side easing that improves corporate profit margins and generates positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The supply-outpaces-inflation signal produces negative 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances.
